text: The chemistry of the interstellar gas and dust is currently studied through numerical models. Such models have been continuously improved in the recent years due to the progress in laboratory studies, which allow for a precise determination of the reaction rate constants under the ISM conditions, and theoretical modelling, which is no longer in equilibrium in star forming regions. In this paper we provide a review of the current status of the dark cloud chemical modelling. By comparing the latest kinetic databases and reaction models, we discuss the ability of the present day models to fit the observed molecular abundances, and analyse the main issues which need to be tackled in order to progress towards a fully consistent theory.
